The Department of Mathematics was started in 2016 together with the establishment of IIT Jammu. The vision of the department is to become a centre of excellence in Mathematics through high quality research and teaching.

**Teaching**

The department is offering various core and elective courses for all B. Tech. streams. Apart from this the department is also offering a minor programme in Mathematics. The minor programme in Mathematics is designed to allow undergraduate students to pursue a more rigorous education in mathematics.

The department has started its PhD program from August 2017. In addition, the department participates in the M. Tech. program in data science and information security.

**Research**

The department has at present energetic and dedicated faculty members in many diverse areas of mathematics. Currently, the main areas o

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

The IIT Jammu Mathematics Colloquium is directed at students, postdocs, and faculty working in the fields of pure and applied Mathematics & Statistics. It aims to present expository lectures or mini-courses that appeal to our wide range of audience at IIT Jammu. One of the main objectives of these colloquia is to inform non-specialists and graduate students about recent research trends, ideas, and results in various areas of Mathematics, rather than focusing on technical details for specialists.

This semester (i.e. 2023-24, Sem-2), the IIT Jammu Mathematics Colloquium will take place every Wednesday afternoon from 3 to 4 pm. Invited external speakers will deliver exciting talks in online mode, while the faculty, graduate students, and the visitors of IIT Jammu will present talks in offline mode in alternate weeks. Please note that the timing for a few talks may be adjusted dependi

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

MathematicsSartaj Ul Hasan

### **Sartaj Ul Hasan**

Associate Professor

#### Brief Info

Sartaj Ul Hasan was born in a remote village in the Shahjahanpur district of Uttar Pradesh, in northern India. He is currently an Associate Professor at IIT Jammu. Prior to joining IIT Jammu, he served as a Scientist at DRDO, Delhi, from February 2007 to January 2018.

From September 2011 to December 2012, he worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher at Carleton University, Ottawa, Canada, where he collaborated with Professor Daniel Panario and Professor Steven Wang. He received his Ph.D. in 2010 from the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay under the supervision of Professor Sudhir R. Ghorpade.

His research interests include various aspects of finite fields and their applications.

For more details, please visit his homepage:

https://sites.google.com/site/sartajulhasan/

#### Personal Website

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

MathematicsSoumyadip Das

### **Soumyadip Das**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

I am an Assistant Professor in Mathematics department, IIT Jammu.

After finishing my integrated MSc from NISER Bhubaneswar I joined ISI Bangalore as a PhD student under Prof. Manish Kumar. My PhD work was on understanding covers of curves in positive characteristic, leading to my dissertation `On the Inertia Conjecture and its Generalizations' that generalizes and provides numerous evidence for a long standing conjecture posed by Abhyankar. I was a visiting fellow at TIFR Mumbai from September 2020 to July 2022. Subsequently, I joined CMI with NBHM in August 2022, and transferred to ISI Kolkata in August 2023.

#### Research Interest

Algebraic Geometry. Specifically, Covers of Curves, Ramification Theory, Etale Fundamental Groups, Orbifold bundles and Moduli Problems.

#### Education Qualificat

#### Publications

1. (With Manish Kumar) On the Inertia Conjecture for Alternating group Covers, J. Pure Appl. Algebra, Volume 224, Issue 9, 106363, 2020.
2. (With Huy Dang, Kostas Karagiannis, Andrew Obus, Vaidehee Thatte) Local Oort Groups and the Isolated Data Criterion Journal des Th\'{e}orie de Nombres Bordeaux, vol. 34, no. 1, 251-269, 2022.
3. (With Indranil Biswas, A. J. Parameswaran) Genuinely ramified maps and stable vector bundles, Int. J. of Mathematics, vol. 33, no. 05, 2250039, 2022.
4. Galois Covers of Singular Curves in Positive Characteristics, To appear in Isr. J. Math, arXiv preprint, 2022.
5. On the Inertia Conjecture and its generalizations, Isr. J. Math., vol. 253, pages 157–204, 2023.
6. Towards the Generalized Purely Wild Inertia Conjecture for product of Alternating and Symmetric Groups, J. Pure Appl. Algebra, vol. 227, 6, 107299, 2023.
7. On the classification of the Covers of Stacky Curves Preserving Slope Stability, Submitted, arXiv preprint, 2022.
8. Modif

MathematicsTanmay Sarkar

### **Tanmay Sarkar**

Associate Professor

#### Brief Info

Heartiest Greetings to my Homepage!

I am a faculty memeber in the department of Mathematics at Indian Institute of Technology  Jammu.

For more details, kindly look into

https://sites.google.com/view/tanmaysarkar/

#### Research Interest

My research interest lies in

- Analysis of Partial Differential Equations
- Numerical Analysis

More specifically, I am interested in

- Hyperbolic Conservation Laws and Numerics
- Nonlinear dispersive equations such as KdV equation
- Discontinuous Galerkin approach to evolution equations
- Inverse problems related to PDEs
- Optimal control of evolution equations

### Message

We started our journey in the year 2016. Over the last few years, we are building lab facilities, growing our expertise and competence in the core Engineering curriculum and research. Faculty is the greatest asset of the department and committed to provide not only the high quality technical education to our students but also the leadership qualities through which they can create employment to others.

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

### About Us

The Department of Mathematics was started in 2016 together with the establishment of IIT Jammu. The vision of the department is to become a centre of excellence in Mathematics through high quality research and teaching. TeachingThe department is offering various core and elective courses for all B. Tech. streams. Apart from this the department is also offering a minor programme in Mathematics.

### Message

We started our journey in the year 2016. Over the last few years, we are building lab facilities, growing our expertise and competence in the core Engineering  curriculum and research. Faculty is the greatest asset of the department and committed to provide not only the high quality technical education to our students but also the leadership qualities through which they can create employment to others.

##### Head of Department | Mathematics

Ajay Kumar is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Associate Professor. Email: ajay.kumar@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - B.Sc., Panjab University, Chandigarh, India.
- M.Sc. (Mathematics), Panjab University, Chandigarh, India.
- Ph.D. (Mathematics), Indian Institute of Science Education and Research, Mohali, India.. Research areas: Commutative Algebra, Combinatorics, Algebra, Graph Theory, Topology, Combinatorial Commutative Algebra. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:Karishma Barman, mathematics:Nimu Basak.

Arvind Kumar is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: arvind.kumar@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - Ph. D. (2017), Harish-Chandra Research Institute, Prayagraj.
- M. Sc. (2012): Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi.
- B. Sc. (2010): B. R. D. P. G. College, Deoria.. Research areas: Number Theory (Modular forms, Siegel modular forms, Galois representations), Modular Forms and Galois Representations, Number theory. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:Anirban Kuila, mathematics:Prabhat Kumar Mishra.

Hiranya Kishore Dey is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: hiranya.dey@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: 1. Ph.D: Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay, 2021.
2. M.Sc: Ramakrishna Mission Vivekananda University, 2015.
3. B.Sc: St. Xavier's College (Autonomous), Kolkata, 2013.. Research areas: Combinatorics, Graph Theory, Finite Group Theory.

Moni Kumari is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: moni.kumari@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: 1. PhD (2018), NISER Bhubaneswar, Odisha.
2. M.Sc. (2012), BHU, Varanasi, UP.
3. B.Sc. (2010), H. D. Jain College, Ara, Bihar.. Research areas: Analytic number theory, Automorphic forms of various types: Maass forms, Hilbert modular forms, Jacobi forms, Siegel modular forms, Automorphic representations, Algebraic Number Theory, Mathematics. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:ADEEN SALI.

Nitin Kumar is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: nitin.kumar@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: PhD, IIT Kharagpur, June 2016-December 2021.

M.Sc., IIT Kharagpur, 2014-2016.

B.Sc., CCS University, Meerut, Uttar Pradesh, 2010-2013.. Research areas: Applied Probability, Stochastic Modelling, Applied Probability. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:Shalini.

Prasant Singh is the Head of Department (HoD) of the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. As HoD, they lead the department's academic and research activities. Designation: Head of Department (HoD). Email: prasant.singh@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - B. Sc. 2005-2008, Udai Pratap Autonomous College Varanasi, (V. B. S. Purvanchal University)
- M. Sc. 2008-2010 Banaras Hindu University
- Ph.D. 2011-2017 IIT Bombay. Research areas: Algebraic Geometric Codes, Finite Geometry, Coding theory. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:Muskan Khaneja, mathematics:Rohit Yadav.

Rahul Dattatraya Kitture is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: rahul.kitture@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: Ph.D. - S.P. Pune University, Pune (2014) and Bhaskaracharya Pratishthana, Pune.. Research areas: My current area of research is in **Group Theory** and **Representation Theory**, Representation Theory, Character theory of finite groups. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:Sakshi Gupta, mathematics:Sonakshee Arora.

Rajiv Kumar is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: rajiv.kumar@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - B.A. Punjabi University Patiala  2005
- B.Ed. Punjabi University Patiala 2007
- M.Sc Mathematics Guru Nanak Dec University Amritsar, 2009
- Ph.D. IIT Bombay 2017. Research areas: Combinatorial Commutative Algebra, Commutative Algebra. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:Paramhans Kushwaha, mathematics:Paromita Bordoloi, mathematics:Sakshi Singh.

Sartaj Ul Hasan is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Associate Professor. Email: sartaj.hasan@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: Ph. D. (Mathematics), IIT Bombay. Research areas: Finite Fields and Their Applications, Finite fields and their applications, Finite Fields and their applications. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:Hridesh Kumar, mathematics:Ramandeep Kaur.

Soumyadip Das is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: soumyadip.das@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: Integrated MSc in Mathematics, 2009-2014, National Institute of Science Education and Research Bhubaneswar, passing year: 2015. PhD in Mathematics, January 2015 - September 2022, Indian Statistical In. Research areas: Algebraic Geometry. Specifically, Covers of Curves, Ramification Theory, Etale Fundamental Groups, Orbifold bundles and Moduli Problems, Algebraic Geometry. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:Bhaskar.

Tanmay Sarkar is a faculty member in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Associate Professor. Email: tanmay.sarkar@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - **B.Sc.:** Ramakrishna Mission Residential College, Narendrapur (2007)
- **M. Sc.:** Indian Institute of Technology Madras (2009)
- **Ph. D.:** Indian Institute of Technology Madras (2014). Research areas: lies in, Analysis of Partial Differential Equations, Numerical Analysis, More specifically, I am interested in, Hyperbolic Conservation Laws and Numerics, Nonlinear dispersive equations such as KdV equation, Discontinuous Galerkin approach to evolution equations. Supervises PhD students: mathematics:Debasish Paul, mathematics:Deepika Garg, mathematics:Nitesh  Kumar.
